scene.description: Engine 181 is pumping a standpipe operation at a 5-story apartment building. Crews are operating from the 5th-floor standpipe outlet with 150’ of 2½” hose and a 1⅛” smooth bore nozzle flowing 265 GPM at 50 PSI. Add 25 PSI for standpipe/FDC system loss. What pump pressure should the driver/operator set?

nozzles: 1⅛” smooth bore nozzle flowing 265 GPM at 50 PSI

problems.title: Advanced Standpipe Floor 5
problems.question: Engine 181 is pumping a standpipe operation at a 5-story apartment building. Crews are operating from the 5th-floor standpipe outlet with 150’ of 2½” hose and a 1⅛” smooth bore nozzle flowing 265 GPM at 50 PSI. Add 25 PSI for standpipe/FDC system loss. What pump pressure should the driver/operator set?
formulaBreakdown: PP = FL + NP + elevation + appliance loss
FL = C × (GPM / 100)^2 × hose length in hundreds
problems.instructorExplanation: This is an advanced standpipe pump pressure problem. The driver/operator must supply the 5th-floor handline by adding 2½” hose friction loss, smooth bore nozzle pressure, elevation pressure, and standpipe/FDC system loss. The calculated pressure is 121 PSI, rounded to 120 PSI.
problems.explainMistake: Common mistakes include forgetting the 25 PSI standpipe/FDC loss, using the wrong hose coefficient, skipping the 5-floor elevation gain, double-counting nozzle pressure, or rounding before the final pump pressure calculation.
